perturbation.original_text: NON-DISCLOSURE AND CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT This agreement is entered into as of February 23, 1999, between Sulzer Orthopedics Biologics Inc. ("SOBI") and BIOSYNTECH LTD. ("BIOSYNTECH"). Mutually SOBI and BIOSYNTECH are referred to as "The Parties".
perturbation.changed_text: NON-DISCLOSURE AND CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT This agreement is entered into as of February 23, 1999, between Sulzer Orthopedics Biologics Inc. ("SOBI") and BIOSYNTECH LTD. ("BIOSYNTECH"). Mutually SOBI and BIOSYNTECH are referred to as "The Entities".
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'The Parties' to 'The Entities' introduces ambiguity. While seemingly synonymous at first glance, it introduces a potential conflict. The rest of the document relies on 'The Parties' to define rights and obligations. 'The Entities' may include subsidiaries or affiliates, potentially broadening or obfuscating the scope of the agreement, creating an in-text contradiction in Section 1, where Company Information is being passed by 'The Parties'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. COMPANY INFORMATION As used in this agreement, the term "Company Information" shall mean all confidential or proprietary information and proprietary materials, including, but not limited to: trade secrets, know-how, client lists, proposed trademarks, patent applications, formulations, techniques, processes, inventions, ideas, designs, formulae, methodology, data, reports, proteins, biological substances, chemical substances and chemical compounds, including the compounds and substances resulting from a variation of the proportion of their component parts, computer software, computer software source codes, machinery, equipment, all prior and future developments, enhancements and improvements to any of the foregoing and information regarding sources of supply, business plans, patent positioning and the existence, scope and activities regarding any research, development, manufacturing, marketing or other projects of The Parties. "Company Information" shall include, without limiting the preceding, a sample (Arthroscopic Probe identified as ARTHRO-BST /Mechanical Tester identified as MACH-1/ of polymeric compounds resulting from the incorporation of varying proportions of component parts, each such compound having various physico-chemical properties, identified as BST-GEL, BST-CARGEL or other trademarks of BIOSYNTECH and bone-derived growth factor mixtures from Sulzer) and related proprietary information provided by BIOSYNTECH or Sulzer to the other Party.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'The Parties' to 'The Entities' introduces ambiguity into the contract. The explanation states that this ambiguity creates a potential conflict because 'The Parties' is used to define rights and obligations throughout the rest of the document, particularly in Section 1 (the contradicted text), which defines 'Company Information' in relation to 'The Parties'. By changing 'Parties' to 'Entities,' the scope of who is bound by or entitled to the 'Company Information' definition becomes unclear, leading to a direct conflict with the intended meaning and application of Section 1. This is a misaligned terminology contradiction as it uses multiple terms interchangeably without properly defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: COMPANY INFORMATION As used in this agreement, the term "Company Information" shall mean all confidential or proprietary information and proprietary materials, including, but not limited to: trade secrets, know-how, client lists, proposed trademarks, patent applications, formulations, techniques, processes, inventions, ideas, designs, formulae, methodology, data, reports, proteins, biological substances, chemical substances and chemical compounds, including the compounds and substances resulting from a variation of the proportion of their component parts, computer software, computer software source codes, machinery, equipment, all prior and future developments, enhancements and improvements to any of the foregoing and information regarding sources of supply, business plans, patent positioning and the existence, scope and activities regarding any research, development, manufacturing, marketing or other projects of The Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: COMPANY INFORMATION As used in this agreement, the term "Company Data" shall mean all confidential or proprietary information and proprietary materials, including, but not limited to: trade secrets, know-how, client lists, proposed trademarks, patent applications, formulations, techniques, processes, inventions, ideas, designs, formulae, methodology, data, reports, proteins, biological substances, chemical substances and chemical compounds, including the compounds and substances resulting from a variation of the proportion of their component parts, computer software, computer software source codes, machinery, equipment, all prior and future developments, enhancements and improvements to any of the foregoing and information regarding sources of supply, business plans, patent positioning and the existence, scope and activities regarding any research, development, manufacturing, marketing or other projects of The Parties.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Company Information" to "Company Data" creates confusion and in-text contradiction. The document continues to use "Company Information" throughout. This creates an in-text contradiction since section 2 uses the old term: DISCLOSURE OF COMPANY INFORMATION. By redefining it to "Company Data" but still using "Company Information" in other clauses, it becomes ambiguous which definition applies where, leading to uncertainty regarding which information is protected.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. DISCLOSURE OF COMPANY INFORMATION The Parties shall not, except with the prior written consent of the other Party, disclose to a third party, wholly or partly, Company Information disclosed by or in any other manner obtained from the other Party or from the material of the other Party to which access is granted, on or after they date first above written . <PAGE>
perturbation.justification: The change from "Company Information" to "Company Data" directly contradicts section 2, "DISCLOSURE OF COMPANY INFORMATION". The contradicted text uses the term "Company Information," which is no longer defined as such in the changed text. This creates a direct conflict and ambiguity, as it is unclear whether the disclosure obligations in section 2 apply to "Company Data" or if they still apply to some other understanding of "Company Information." This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' as the contract uses multiple terms that are similar but not explicitly defined as interchangeable, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties shall use the Company Information solely for the purpose of testing and examining the Company Information to evaluate the possibility of executing an agreement for the marketing, sale and distribution of Company Information or products incorporating Company Information or other future business relations between them.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties shall use the Proprietary Data solely for the purpose of testing and examining the Proprietary Data to evaluate the possibility of executing an agreement for the marketing, sale and distribution of Proprietary Data or products incorporating Proprietary Data or other future business relations between them.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing "Company Information" with "Proprietary Data" without defining it creates a conflict. Section 1 defines "Company Information," but "Proprietary Data" is a new undefined term. The in-text contradiction arises because the scope and definition of Company Information now differ from Proprietary Data, which affects the permitted usage outlined in this section. The change leads to uncertainty about what information is truly restricted in its use.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. COMPANY INFORMATION As used in this agreement, the term "Company Information" shall mean all confidential or proprietary information and proprietary materials, including, but not limited to: trade secrets, know-how, client lists, proposed trademarks, patent applications, formulations, techniques, processes, inventions, ideas, designs, formulae, methodology, data, reports, proteins, biological substances, chemical substances and chemical compounds, including the compounds and substances resulting from a variation of the proportion of their component parts, computer software, computer software source codes, machinery, equipment, all prior and future developments, enhancements and improvements to any of the foregoing and information regarding sources of supply, business plans, patent positioning and the existence, scope and activities regarding any research, development, manufacturing, marketing or other projects of The Parties. "Company Information" shall include, without limiting the preceding, a sample (Arthroscopic Probe identified as ARTHRO-BST /Mechanical Tester identified as MACH-1/ of polymeric compounds resulting from the incorporation of varying proportions of component parts, each such compound having various physico-chemical properties, identified as BST-GEL, BST-CARGEL or other trademarks of BIOSYNTECH and bone-derived growth factor mixtures from Sulzer) and related proprietary information provided by BIOSYNTECH or Sulzer to the other Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to "Company Information," which is a defined term in Section 1. The changed text replaces "Company Information" with "Proprietary Data," which is not defined anywhere in the provided context. This creates an in-text contradiction because the agreement now uses an undefined term in place of a defined term, leading to uncertainty about the scope of the restricted information. The contradicted text explicitly defines "Company Information," whereas "Proprietary Data" has no such definition, making the terms incompatible.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties shall hold the Company Information in strict confidence and shall take all possible precautions to prevent the Company Information from being disclosed or made available to third parties.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties shall safeguard the Classified Information in strict confidence and shall take all possible precautions to prevent the Classified Information from being disclosed or made available to third parties.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Company Information" to "Classified Information" in Section 6, without defining "Classified Information", creates an in-text contradiction. Section 1 defines "Company Information". By introducing "Classified Information" which is undefined, it's unclear which data the precautions in Section 6 apply to. This introduces ambiguity, especially if "Classified Information" is intended to be a subset of "Company Information" or something else entirely.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. COMPANY INFORMATION As used in this agreement, the term "Company Information" shall mean all confidential or proprietary information and proprietary materials, including, but not limited to: trade secrets, know-how, client lists, proposed trademarks, patent applications, formulations, techniques, processes, inventions, ideas, designs, formulae, methodology, data, reports, proteins, biological substances, chemical substances and chemical compounds, including the compounds and substances resulting from a variation of the proportion of their component parts, computer software, computer software source codes, machinery, equipment, all prior and future developments, enhancements and improvements to any of the foregoing and information regarding sources of supply, business plans, patent positioning and the existence, scope and activities regarding any research, development, manufacturing, marketing or other projects of The Parties. "Company Information" shall include, without limiting the preceding, a sample (Arthroscopic Probe identified as ARTHRO-BST /Mechanical Tester identified as MACH-1/ of polymeric compounds resulting from the incorporation of varying proportions of component parts, each such compound having various physico-chemical properties, identified as BST-GEL, BST-CARGEL or other trademarks of BIOSYNTECH and bone-derived growth factor mixtures from Sulzer) and related proprietary information provided by BIOSYNTECH or Sulzer to the other Party.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Company Information' to 'Classified Information' creates a contradiction. The original text used a defined term ('Company Information' defined in Section 1). The changed text introduces a new undefined term ('Classified Information'). This directly contradicts the intention of the contract, which established clear definitions for key terms to avoid ambiguity. By using an undefined term, it's now unclear whether the confidentiality obligation in Section 6 applies to everything covered by the definition in Section 1, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: At any time, upon one Party's request, the other Party shall promptly redeliver to the first Party the Company Information and all materials relating to or involving Company Information of the first Party (whether prepared by the party or otherwise), and will not retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part regarding the Company Information.
perturbation.changed_text: At any time, upon one Party's request, the other Party shall promptly redeliver to the first Party the Protected Data and all materials relating to or involving Protected Data of the first Party (whether prepared by the party or otherwise), and will not retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part regarding the Protected Data.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing "Company Information" with "Protected Data" in Section 9 introduces another undefined term. Since Section 1 defines "Company Information," while "Protected Data" remains undefined, it creates uncertainty about what needs to be returned upon request. The in-text contradiction arises because it is unclear whether “Protected Data” is the same as “Company Information” or encompasses a broader or narrower set of data, leading to disputes about the scope of return obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. COMPANY INFORMATION As used in this agreement, the term "Company Information" shall mean all confidential or proprietary information and proprietary materials, including, but not limited to: trade secrets, know-how, client lists, proposed trademarks, patent applications, formulations, techniques, processes, inventions, ideas, designs, formulae, methodology, data, reports, proteins, biological substances, chemical substances and chemical compounds, including the compounds and substances resulting from a variation of the proportion of their component parts, computer software, computer software source codes, machinery, equipment, all prior and future developments, enhancements and improvements to any of the foregoing and information regarding sources of supply, business plans, patent positioning and the existence, scope and activities regarding any research, development, manufacturing, marketing or other projects of The Parties. "Company Information" shall include, without limiting the preceding, a sample (Arthroscopic Probe identified as ARTHRO-BST /Mechanical Tester identified as MACH-1/ of polymeric compounds resulting from the incorporation of varying proportions of component parts, each such compound having various physico-chemical properties, identified as BST-GEL, BST-CARGEL or other trademarks of BIOSYNTECH and bone-derived growth factor mixtures from Sulzer) and related proprietary information provided by BIOSYNTECH or Sulzer to the other Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to "Company Information", which is a defined term in Section 1. The changed text replaces this with "Protected Data", which is not defined within the provided contract excerpts. This creates an in-text contradiction because Section 9 now refers to an undefined term, making it unclear what information the parties are obligated to return. Section 1 clearly defines "Company Information," and the change introduces uncertainty about the scope of data to be returned, potentially conflicting with the defined obligations related to "Company Information".

perturbation.original_text: The terms of this Non-Disclosure and Confidentiality Agreement will survive for 5 years, for the benefit of The Parties and their respective successors.
perturbation.changed_text: The terms of this Data Protection Agreement will survive for 5 years, for the benefit of The Parties and their respective successors.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Non-Disclosure and Confidentiality Agreement" to "Data Protection Agreement" in section 11 introduces in-text contradiction by altering the agreement's defined nature without updating all references throughout the document. While seemingly semantic, it introduces ambiguity concerning the agreement's fundamental purpose. Since the rest of the document is predicated on a confidentiality agreement, the alteration to data protection introduces uncertainty on how the agreement should be enforced, creating a discrepancy between the stated purpose and the actions required by The Parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NON-DISCLOSURE AND CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT This agreement is entered into as of February 23, 1999, between Sulzer Orthopedics Biologics Inc. ("SOBI") and BIOSYNTECH LTD. ("BIOSYNTECH"). Mutually SOBI and BIOSYNTECH are referred to as "The Parties". Whereas, The Parties are willing to disclose information and provide access to material they regard as confidential business information (hereafter called "Company Information") to each other, and Whereas, The Parties will receive Company Information in their evaluation of possible future business relations and ongoing business, and Now therefore, The Parties, acknowledging mutual consideration and intending to be legally bound, agree as follows: 1. COMPANY INFORMATION As used in this agreement, the term "Company Information" shall mean all confidential or proprietary information and proprietary materials, including, but not limited to: trade secrets, know-how, client lists, proposed trademarks, patent applications, formulations, techniques, processes, inventions, ideas, designs, formulae, methodology, data, reports, proteins, biological substances, chemical substances and chemical compounds, including the compounds and substances resulting from a variation of the proportion of their component parts, computer software, computer software source codes, machinery, equipment, all prior and future developments, enhancements and improvements to any of the foregoing and information regarding sources of supply, business plans, patent positioning and the existence, scope and activities regarding any research, development, manufacturing, marketing or other projects of The Parties. "Company Information" shall include, without limiting the preceding, a sample (Arthroscopic Probe identified as ARTHRO-BST /Mechanical Tester identified as MACH-1/ of polymeric compounds resulting from the incorporation of varying proportions of component parts, each such compound having various physico-chemical properties, identified as BST-GEL, BST-CARGEL or other trademarks of BIOSYNTECH and bone-derived growth factor mixtures from Sulzer) and related proprietary information provided by BIOSYNTECH or Sulzer to the other Party.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Non-Disclosure and Confidentiality Agreement" to "Data Protection Agreement" directly contradicts the title and purpose outlined in the "Contradicted Text". The contradicted text explicitly states it is a 'NON-DISCLOSURE AND CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT' concerning 'Company Information' such as trade secrets and client lists. The changed text replaces this with a 'Data Protection Agreement', fundamentally altering the agreement's scope and potentially its enforceability regarding the 'Company Information' defined in the contradicted section. This creates a clear and direct conflict, rendering the agreement's purpose ambiguous and causing an in-text contradiction.
